g.text: Input: Part 1 results (from MongoDB)
for each applicable LOB: which requirements apply to it
for each requirement: section it belongs to and its full text
LOB profile text (name, responsibilities, scope) also in MongoDB
change description from the original notification
P2 Step 1: Loop through each impacted LOB
read the list of impacted LOBs from MongoDB
for each LOB: retrieve its profile text and applicable requirements
Steps 2 through 7 repeat once for every impacted LOB
P2 Step 2: Embed the LOB profile text
input: LOB profile text from MongoDB
profile includes name, description, responsibilities and scope
output: a vector representing the meaning of this LOB's responsibilities

svg.text: input: LOB profile vector from Step 2
searching against: Index 1 (the policy index, not the LOB index)
metadata filter 1: policy_id equals the current policy being analyzed
Type A requirement chunks excluded: we already know which reqs apply
output: top-K document sections most similar to this LOB profile

g.text: P2 Step 4: Re-rank retrieved sections by requirement relevance
input: top-K document sections from Step 3
score each section against every applicable requirement for this LOB
sections close to the LOB but unrelated to any requirement score low
sections related to both the LOB and a requirement score high
output: re-ranked list, top 8 most relevant sections kept
MongoDB: top document sections stored for this LOB
stores the re-ranked top document sections for this LOB
each section carries: doc_title, section_heading_path, chunk text,
link_type, and which requirement it scored highest against

svg.text: P2 Step 6: LLM synthesis call (one call per impacted LOB)
Input A: change description from the original notification
Input B: LOB profile (name, responsibilities, scope) from MongoDB
Input C: list of requirements applicable to this LOB with section context
Input D: top 8 re-ranked document sections from MongoDB (Step 5)
Task: for each document section this LOB must act on, return:
what specifically this LOB must do
which requirement number this action addresses
Output: structured list of action items for this LOB

g.text: P2 Step 7: Write action item list to MongoDB
store: LOB name, action items, doc title, section heading,
requirement number addressed, and timestamp
MongoDB is the final destination for all Part 2 results
Part 2 complete: output per impacted LOB in MongoDB
for each impacted LOB: a structured list of action items
each item names: the document, the section, what must be done,
and which requirement number it addresses
all results stored in MongoDB for downstream workflows

svg.text: Why Part 2 uses so few LLM calls
Old approach: one call per LOB per requirement per supporting document
Example: 3 LOBs x 10 reqs x 200 docs = 6,000 calls
New approach: one LLM call per impacted LOB
Example: 3 LOBs = 3 calls, vector search narrows docs before LLM sees them
